findings.title: Hardcoded API Key Environment Variable Reference
findings.description: The Anthropic API key is referenced directly from environment variables without proper validation or fallback handling. If the environment variable is not set, the application will fail with potential information disclosure.
findings.suggestion: Add proper environment variable validation and consider using a secure secrets management system. Validate the API key exists before creating the client instance.

findings.title: Hardcoded Private Key Environment Variable Reference
findings.description: The agent private key is loaded directly from environment variables without validation. This could lead to application crashes or unexpected behavior if not properly set, and represents a critical security dependency.
findings.suggestion: Implement proper validation for the AGENT_PRIVATE_KEY environment variable, use secure key management practices, and add error handling for missing or malformed keys.

findings.title: Insufficient Input Validation in GitHub Tool Functions
findings.description: The GitHub tool functions accept user input for owner, repo, and path parameters without proper validation. This could lead to injection attacks or unintended API calls to malicious repositories.
findings.suggestion: Add input validation and sanitization for GitHub repository parameters. Validate repository names, paths, and implement allowlists for trusted repositories.

findings.title: Missing Rate Limiting on API Endpoints
findings.description: The /api/agent-log endpoint and other API routes lack rate limiting, which could lead to denial of service attacks or excessive resource consumption.
findings.suggestion: Implement rate limiting middleware for all API endpoints using libraries like express-rate-limit or built-in Next.js rate limiting solutions.

findings.title: Insecure File System Access
findings.description: The agent-log API endpoint reads files from the filesystem using user-controllable paths without proper validation, potentially leading to path traversal vulnerabilities.
findings.suggestion: Implement proper path validation and sanitization. Use path.resolve() and validate that the resolved path is within allowed directories.

findings.title: Missing Error Handling in Blockchain Operations
findings.description: Blockchain operations in ERC8004 functions may throw unhandled exceptions, potentially exposing sensitive information about the system state or causing application crashes.
findings.suggestion: Add comprehensive try-catch blocks around blockchain operations and implement proper error logging without exposing sensitive details to users.

findings.title: Insecure HTTP Client for External API Calls
findings.description: The agent makes HTTP requests to external services without proper timeout, retry logic, or certificate validation, which could lead to hanging requests or man-in-the-middle attacks.
findings.suggestion: Implement proper HTTP client configuration with timeouts, retry policies, and certificate validation. Consider using a more robust HTTP client library.

findings.title: Missing Input Sanitization in Agent Prompts
findings.description: User input is passed directly to AI model prompts without proper sanitization, which could lead to prompt injection attacks or unexpected model behavior.
findings.suggestion: Implement input sanitization and validation for all user-provided data before including it in AI prompts. Use parameterized prompts where possible.

findings.title: Verbose Error Messages
findings.description: Error handling in several functions may expose internal system information through verbose error messages, potentially aiding attackers in reconnaissance.
findings.suggestion: Implement proper error handling that logs detailed errors internally but returns generic error messages to users.

findings.title: Missing Security Headers Configuration
findings.description: The Next.js configuration does not include security headers like CSP, HSTS, or X-Frame-Options, which could leave the application vulnerable to various client-side attacks.
findings.suggestion: Add security headers configuration in next.config.ts including Content Security Policy, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, and other relevant security headers.

findings.title: Missing TypeScript Strict Mode Configuration
findings.description: While strict mode is enabled, additional TypeScript strict checks could be enabled for better type safety and code quality.
findings.suggestion: Consider enabling additional strict TypeScript options like noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, and noImplicitReturns for enhanced type safety.

findings.title: Lack of Input Schema Validation
findings.description: The application defines input schemas but doesn't appear to validate incoming data against these schemas at runtime, which could lead to runtime errors.
findings.suggestion: Implement runtime schema validation using libraries like Zod or Joi to ensure data integrity and type safety at application boundaries.

findings.title: Missing Logging Security Best Practices
findings.description: The logging system may inadvertently log sensitive information without proper sanitization or classification of log levels.
findings.suggestion: Implement log sanitization to prevent logging of sensitive data and add proper log level classification for security-related events.
